codeReview.summary: fleet_drift appears to meet the feature contract, but fleet_rollback does not actually enforce the promised HEAD verification step. A host can be reported as successful even when reading HEAD after checkout fails, so VAL-MCP-009 is not fully satisfied.

issues.description: After checkoutRef succeeds, getHead errors are swallowed and converted to undefined, then the host is still reported with status:"ok". The feature description and VAL-MCP-009 require rollback to verify and return the resulting HEAD per host, so a post-checkout HEAD failure must not be surfaced as a successful rollback.

The added rollback tests cover checkout success, checkout failure, all-host failure, and host filtering, but they never simulate the required verification edge case where checkout succeeds and getHead fails afterward. That missing test allowed the success-without-head regression in server.ts to pass unnoticed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mcp-worker skill's manual verification procedure only exercises initialize/tools-list, so workers can satisfy the skill without actually invoking the new MCP tool they implemented. That left this feature's required MCP-level drift/rollback behavior unchecked outside unit tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mcp-worker/SKILL.md manual verification section only lists initialize and tools/list examples; this worker's handoff verification only records `printf '..initialize..\n..tools/list..\n' | node apps/mcp-server/dist/index.js`, while features.json for mcp-drift-rollback-tools expects manual MCP drift testing.

summary: Review failed: fleet_rollback reports success even when HEAD verification cannot be completed after checkout, which violates the feature's promised 'resulting HEAD per host' behavior. I also noted a test gap for that scenario and a skill-level manual-verification gap that helped the issue slip through.
